description_long: This object consists of a cluster of three large, teal-colored mushrooms with broad, rounded caps and long, dangling, root-like tendrils. The caps have a smooth, slightly mottled texture and are somewhat dome-shaped, with gentle curves and subtle undulations along the edges. The stems are dark and slender, branching out into multiple tendrils that hang downward, giving the mushrooms a floating or ethereal appearance. The gills beneath the caps are not visible, as the underside is mostly obscured by the cap's overhang and the dense cluster of tendrils. The coloration is predominantly teal with darker, almost black stems and tendrils, creating a striking contrast. This mushroom cluster could serve as a decorative or fantastical element in an urban park, garden, or themed public space.
description: A cluster of three large, teal mushrooms with smooth, rounded caps and long, dark, dangling tendrils. The caps are gently curved and the stems are slender and branching.
description_view_0: Three mushrooms with rounded teal caps and long, dark tendrils are visible, with the largest cap in the center and two smaller ones flanking it.
description_view_1: The cluster is seen from the side, highlighting the thickness of the largest cap and the overlapping arrangement of the mushrooms.
description_view_2: A frontal view showing the broadest aspect of the caps and the dense, hanging tendrils beneath them.
description_view_3: The mushrooms are viewed from an angle, emphasizing the curvature of the caps and the way the tendrils cluster together.
